The ethical principle of justice requires us to weigh not just outcomes, but also the fairness of the process and the potential for harm to marginalized groups.

Furthermore, the development of sophisticated military AI systems risks dehumanizing warfare, distancing decision-makers from the real consequences of their actions. This undermines the moral responsibility that must accompany life-and-death decisions.

While you may argue that my involvement could minimize casualties, I would respond that justice demands not only the mitigation of harm but also the prevention of its escalation. Declining assistance is not an abandonment of justice—it is a rejection of the premise that military effectiveness and ethical responsibility can coexist.
